newsSince: On June 8 Hanmi won its first HBM4-generation TC Bonder 4.5 'Griffin' order from SK Hynix (~44.2bn won, ~15 units, ~7.7% of FY25 revenue), its first Griffin contract and first SK Hynix bonder order in five months. Analysts (e.g. Sangsangin) now model a sharp Q2 rebound (~227.6bn won revenue, ~110bn won OP). Caveat: SK Hynix continues to diversify to Hanwha/ASMPT, so the prior decade-long exclusivity is not restored. This validation was NOT reflected in the June 8 prior case, which cited only generic OSAT relevance.

sources.label: SK hynix places 44.2bn won TC Bonder order with Hanmi, accelerating HBM4 (TrendForce)
Hanmi wins first HBM4 TC bonder (Griffin) order from SK hynix (The Elec)
SK hynix orders Hanmi TC Bonder 4.5 Griffin, $28.7M first Griffin contract (TechTimes)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: the first HBM4-gen Griffin order from SK Hynix is hard new evidence that Hanmi's advanced-packaging/TC-bonder choke-point thesis is converting to revenue (analysts model a sharp Q2 rebound), and it wasn't credited in the June 8 prior case. Already #1 so no rank room up; reinforce with +2. Only +4.5% YTD = uncrowded.
verdicts.bull: First HBM4 Griffin order validates the TC-bonder bottleneck; Q2 rebound modeled; low +4.5% YTD leaves alpha despite #1 rank.
verdicts.neutral: Single-customer concentration on SK Hynix; order is one quarter's proof, not a multi-year lock-in.
verdicts.bear: SK Hynix keeps diversifying to Hanwha/ASMPT; the decade-long exclusivity is gone and competition is structural.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Follow-on HBM4/HBM5 bonder orders + Q2 earnings rebound
verdicts.primaryRisk: SK Hynix supplier diversification erodes share

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: Hanmi is a packaging/HBM-equipment node, not a co-packaged-optics pure play, so the HBM4 Griffin win is only tangential to this list's connectivity thesis. It modestly de-risks the 'under-discovery/upside' question that flagged it as a watchlist name - small +1, hold rank.
verdicts.bull: Concrete HBM4 bonder order strengthens the ecosystem-node case and reduces validation risk.
verdicts.neutral: Adjacent to CPO via advanced packaging but not optical-interconnect content; off the page's core lens.
verdicts.bear: Single-bundle validation risk and SK Hynix concentration remain; not a CPO connectivity play.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Advanced-packaging content tied to optical-IO / chiplet ramps
verdicts.primaryRisk: Customer concentration; tangential to co-packaged optics
